BIS Awareness Meet for the MSME Sector on RO Water Treatment/Dispensing System
articles.full_content:
The Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS), Coimbatore Branch Office, in association with the Southern Region Water Treatment Equipment’s Manufacturers and Traders (SRWTEMT) Association, successfully organized a BIS Awareness Meet for MSME Sector focused on RO Water Treatment/Dispensing System at POPPYS Hotel, Madurai.
The meet aimed to create awareness among MSMEs and manufacturers in the water treatment sector about Indian Standards, mandatory Quality Control Orders (QCO), and BIS licensing procedures for RO Water Treatment/Dispensing Systems. The program also covered BIS activities, compliance requirements, and the importance of standardization to ensure product quality, safety, and consumer confidence.
Shri. Ramesh K, Scientist-E/ Director and Head, BIS Coimbatore, delivered the Welcome Address and outlined the programme objectives.
Shri Krishna Kumar and Shri Mahendran, Executive Committee Members of SRWTEMT Association, along with Shri Srinivasan from Three Ply Apple Aqua, Madurai, addressed the gathering and shared industry perspectives and challenges.
As part of the proceedings, BIS officials felicitated association members and BIS licensees for their contribution to quality and compliance in the sector.
Shri. Gautam Chand Soni, Scientist-C/ Deputy Director, BIS Coimbatore, presented an overview of BIS activities. This was followed by a key technical session on Indian Standards, QCO and BIS Licensing Procedures for RO Water Treatment/Dispensing System delivered by Shri. Rino John S, Scientist-E/ Director, BIS Coimbatore.
An Open House Interaction was held for participants to engage with BIS officials and industry experts on regulatory and technical queries.
The Bureau of Indian Standards reiterated its commitment to supporting MSMEs in achieving quality compliance and consumer trust through standardization and certification.
articles.summary: The Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS), Coimbatore Branch Office, in association with the Southern Region Water Treatment Equipment’s Manufacturers and Traders (SRWTEMT) Association, successfully organized a BIS Awareness Meet for MSME Sector focused on RO Water Treatment/Dispensing System at POPPYS Hotel, Madurai. The meet aimed to create awareness among MSMEs and manufacturers in the water treatment sector about Indian Standards, mandatory Quality Control Orders (QCO), and BIS licensing procedures for RO Water Treatment/Dispensing Systems. The program also covered BIS activities, compliance requirements, and the importance of standardization to ensure product quality, safety, and consumer confidence.

MSME-DFO Gangtok Holds Awareness Programme on Marketing & Branding for PM Vishwakarma Artisans at Pakyong
articles.full_content:
Pakyong | 09-07-2026
The MSME-Development & Facilitation Office, Gangtok, under the Ministry of MSME, Govt. of India, in collaboration with the District Administration, Pakyong, organized a One-Day Awareness Programme on Marketing and Branding under the PM Vishwakarma Scheme at Pakyong on July 9, 2026.
The objective of the programme was to strengthen the entrepreneurial skills of PM Vishwakarma artisans by providing training on branding, packaging, digital marketing, financial literacy, export avenues, artificial intelligence, and market linkages.
Shri Sangay Gyatso Bhutia, SCS, ADC (Development), Pakyong, attended as the Chief Guest. The programme began with the felicitation of dignitaries, followed by the lighting of the inaugural lamp and the recitation of the National Anthem.
In his welcome address, Shri Nirmal Chowdhury, Assistant Director & Head of Office, MSME-DFO, Gangtok, expressed gratitude to the dignitaries and participants and outlined the purpose of the programme. Shri Sudhir Kanekar, DGM, RBI, Gangtok, spoke on financial inclusion and digital banking. In his address, the Chief Guest, Shri Sangay Bhutia, urged artisans to uphold traditional craftsmanship while adopting branding, innovation, and digital tools.
The inaugural session concluded with the presentation of mementos and a Vote of Thanks by Dr. Rahul Kamble, Assistant Director, MSME-DFO, Gangtok.
In the technical session, Dr. P. K. Das, Assistant Professor, Sikkim University, spoke on ‘Strategic Branding, Packaging and Pricing for PM Vishwakarma Artisans’. Ms. Pasang Sherpa, Lead District Manager, Pakyong, briefed participants on PM Vishwakarma loan procedures. Shri Sameer Pradhan, India Post, spoke on Dak Ghar Niryat Kendras (DNKs). Shri Sohan Singh Rajpurohit, Manager, RBI, Gangtok, conducted a session on financial awareness. Shri Jaydeep Chakraborty and Shri Amit Tamang from the SLPMU Team, Sikkim, demonstrated the application of Artificial Intelligence in design, marketing, and business growth for PM Vishwakarma crafts. Ms. Rinzen Choden Bhutia, Founder & Director, Trateng Greens, also shared her entrepreneurial success story.
The programme concluded with a Vote of Thanks by Shri Nirmal Chowdhury. The event witnessed active participation from PM Vishwakarma artisans and other stakeholders, reflecting the Government of India’s commitment to empowering traditional artisans through skill development, financial support, technology adoption, and better market access.
@MSMEDFOGANGTOK under @minmsme in collaboration with the District Administration, Pakyong, organized a One-Day Awareness Programme today on Marketing and Branding under the PM Vishwakarma Scheme at Pakyong. Details: https://t.co/28vBvu36vv @minmsme @PIB_India @PIB_Guwahati pic.twitter.com/9oTbdPTwdl
articles.summary: Pakyong | 09-07-2026 The MSME-Development & Facilitation Office, Gangtok, under the Ministry of MSME, Govt. of India, in collaboration with the District Administration, Pakyong, organized a One-Day Awareness Programme on Marketing and Branding under the PM Vishwakarma Scheme at Pakyong on July 9, 2026. The objective of the programme was to strengthen the entrepreneurial skills of PM Vishwakarma artisans by providing training on branding, packaging, digital marketing, financial literacy, export avenues, artificial intelligence, and market linkages.

Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia Invites Investors to Explore Tripura’s Vast Growth Potential at Destination Tripura Business Conclave 2026
articles.full_content:
Tripura Emerging as India’s Gateway To Southeast Asia Under PM Modi’s Act East Vision: Scindia Scindia Praises CM Manik Saha's Leadership in Building a Connected and Prosperous Tripura Tripura Emerges as India's Gateway to Southeast Asia and a New Investment Destination: Scindia
Union Minister for Development of North Eastern Region and Communications, Jyotiraditya M. Scindia, today addressed the Destination Tripura Business Conclave 2026 virtually and urged industry leaders to partner in Tripura’s remarkable growth story, describing the State as one of the Northeast’s most promising investment destinations.
Union Minister Scindia asserted that the under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, the Northeast is emerging as the engine of growth that will play a defining role in the journey towards a Viksit Bharat. He noted that the Prime Minister’s transformative Act East Policy has repositioned the region from India’s frontier to its gateway to Southeast Asia, unlocking unprecedented opportunities for trade, investment and regional connectivity.
Describing Tripura as standing at the intersection of connectivity, commerce and opportunity, the Minister said the Destination Tripura Business Conclave showcases a State that is ready to become a preferred destination for investors seeking long-term opportunities. He said Agartala is emerging as a strategic gateway to Southeast Asia, connecting businesses to the ASEAN region of over 670 million people and one of the world’s largest economic blocs.
Union Minister of DoNER highlighted Tripura’s exceptional strengths, including abundant reserves of natural gas, bamboo, agarwood, rubber, tea, spices and horticultural produce, a highly educated workforce, investor-friendly policies, expanding infrastructure, and India’s third International Internet Gateway. He noted that Tripura’s emergence as India’s largest bamboo-producing State and the country’s second-largest producer of natural rubber offers immense opportunities for manufacturing, food processing, value addition, logistics, exports and technology-led industries.
Emphasising that “investment follows confidence, confidence follows infrastructure, and infrastructure follows vision”, Scindia said the Government of India has fundamentally transformed the Northeast’s development landscape over the past decade through enhanced connectivity, modern infrastructure and strong institutional support. He highlighted key initiatives such as the India–Myanmar–Thailand Trilateral Highway, the Kaladan Multimodal Transit Transport Project, the Special Economic Zone at Sabroom, the Maitri Setu bridge and the Integrated Check Post, all of which are positioning Tripura as a springboard for exports to Southeast Asia.
Calling upon investors to seize the opportunities before them, Scindia said Tripura’s transformation from a landlocked State into a gateway to the Bay of Bengal reflects the larger transformation of the Northeast under Prime Minister Narendra
articles.summary: Tripura Emerging as India’s Gateway To Southeast Asia Under PM Modi’s Act East Vision: Scindia Scindia Praises CM Manik Saha's Leadership in Building a Connected and Prosperous Tripura Tripura Emerges as India's Gateway to Southeast Asia and a New Investment Destination: Scindia Union Minister for Development of North Eastern Region and Communications, Jyotiraditya M. Scindia, today addressed the Destination Tripura Business Conclave 2026 virtually and urged industry leaders to partner in Tripura’s remarkable growth story, describing the State as one of the Northeast’s most promising investment destinations. Union Minister Scindia asserted that the under the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, the Northeast is emerging as the engine of growth that will play a defining role in the journey towards a Viksit Bharat.

DPIIT Amends Footwear Quality Control Orders to Enhance Ease of Doing Business and Strengthen Domestic Manufacturing
articles.full_content:
DPIIT Extends Legacy Stock Clearance Timeline for Footwear Till 31 July 2027 DPIIT Permits Import of Up to 4,500 Footwear Samples Annually for Research and Development
The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) has introduced amendments to two Quality Control Orders (QCOs) relating to the footwear sector with the objective of facilitating ease of doing business and strengthening the domestic footwear manufacturing ecosystem. The amendments were notified on 12 June 2026 vide S.O. 3038(E) and S.O. 3037(E), respectively, pertaining to the Footwear made from Leather and other Materials (Quality Control) Order, 2024, and the Footwear made from All Rubber and all Polymeric Material and its Components (Quality Control) Order, 2024.
Under the amendments, the timeline for clearance of legacy stock has been extended from 31 July 2026 to 31 July 2027. As footwear products are largely seasonal and inventories often remain within the supply chain beyond a single selling cycle, the additional one-year period will provide manufacturers, distributors and retailers adequate time for the orderly liquidation of existing stock. The extension is expected to reduce the compliance burden, minimise disruption to trade and ensure that only BIS-certified footwear products are sold in the market thereafter.
The amendments also provide an exemption for the import of samples for Research & Development (R&D). Manufacturers of leather and footwear products may import up to 4,500 pairs of footwear annually for research and development and other non-commercial purposes. Such samples shall not be sold commercially, must be prominently marked and embossed with the words "NOT FOR SALE", and shall be disposed of as scrap after use. Manufacturers will be required to maintain year-wise records of such imports and furnish the same to the Government as and when required.
The sample exemption has been introduced to enable manufacturers to evaluate product designs, assess whether documentation alone is sufficient for product assessment, and determine the quantity of samples genuinely required for research and development. Samples imported under this provision are intended exclusively for vendor presentations or for replication and subsequent manufacture within India and are expressly prohibited from commercial sale.
These amendments reflect DPIIT's continued commitment to strengthening India's quality ecosystem while promoting ease of doing business. The transitional provisions, including the extension of the legacy stock clearance deadline until 31 July 2027 and the annual import of up to 4,500 pairs of footwear samples for research and development, are intended to ease compliance, support product development and innovation, and facilitate industry operations without compromising quality standards. The measures are aligned with the Prime Minister's vision of "Zero Defect, Zero Effect" manufacturing and are expected to advance the objectives of the Make in India i
articles.summary: DPIIT Extends Legacy Stock Clearance Timeline for Footwear Till 31 July 2027 DPIIT Permits Import of Up to 4,500 Footwear Samples Annually for Research and Development The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) has introduced amendments to two Quality Control Orders (QCOs) relating to the footwear sector with the objective of facilitating ease of doing business and strengthening the domestic footwear manufacturing ecosystem. The amendments were notified on 12 June 2026 vide S.O. 3037(E), respectively, pertaining to the Footwear made from Leather and other Materials (Quality Control) Order, 2024, and the Footwear made from All Rubber and all Polymeric Material and its Components (Quality Control) Order, 2024.
